A test to verify that `String.prototype` is not enumerable in the `String` object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that iterates over the properties of the `String` object in JavaScript. It checks if any property is equal to `String.prototype`. If such a property is found, the script calls the `assert` function with `false`, which typically indicates a failed assertion in testing contexts. This script provides narrow functionality, likely intended to verify that `String.prototype` is not directly enumerable as a property of the `String` object.
